title: Uncapped indemnification — RED FLAG
standard_language: Each party agrees to indemnify the other for direct damages arising from breach, subject to the cap on liability set forth in Section X.
red_flag: Customer shall indemnify Vendor for any and all losses, including consequential, special, and punitive damages, without limitation.
why_matters: Uncapped indemnity exposes the company to unlimited liability — bankruptcy risk on a single dispute. Standard cap = 12 months fees OR 2× annual contract value.
suggested_redline: Replace 'without limitation' with 'up to a cap equal to twelve (12) months of fees paid under this Agreement.' Add carve-outs only for: gross negligence, willful misconduct, IP infringement, breach of confidentiality.

title: IP assignment of pre-existing IP — RED FLAG
standard_language: Contractor assigns to Company all right, title, and interest in deliverables created hereunder. Contractor retains pre-existing IP and grants Company a perpetual license to such pre-existing IP solely as necessary to use the Deliverables.
red_flag: Contractor irrevocably assigns to Company all intellectual property, including any pre-existing IP used in connection with the Services.
why_matters: Assigning pre-existing IP would give the customer ownership of the vendor's existing platform / library — vendor cannot sell to other customers. Industry-standard is assign-deliverables-only + license-back of pre-existing IP.
suggested_redline: Insert 'EXCEPT FOR pre-existing IP, which remains the sole property of Contractor. Contractor grants Company a perpetual, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such pre-existing IP solely to the extent necessary to use the Deliverables.'

title: Unilateral termination for convenience — RED FLAG (vendor side)
standard_language: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
red_flag: Customer may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any or no reason, upon five (5) days' notice. Customer shall have no liability for any wind-down costs.
why_matters: 5-day notice with no wind-down liability prevents vendor from recouping investment in customer onboarding / staffing. Standard is 60-90 day notice with payment for work performed.
suggested_redline: Replace '5 days' with '90 days' written notice. Add: 'Customer shall pay for all Services performed, all approved expenses incurred, and any non-cancellable commitments made by Vendor through the termination date.'

title: Missing residual-knowledge clause — RED FLAG
standard_language: Either party may use Residuals (information retained in the unaided memory of personnel who had access to the Confidential Information) without restriction, provided that such use does not infringe the other party's intellectual property rights or breach this Agreement.
red_flag: Receiving party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. No exceptions for residual knowledge.
why_matters: Without a residuals clause, employees who saw confidential information are effectively prohibited from working in the same industry. Standard practice is to permit residuals (knowledge in unaided memory) with IP carve-outs.
suggested_redline: Add: 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may use Residuals (information retained in the unaided memory of its personnel) for any purpose, provided that such use does not infringe the other party's intellectual property rights.'

title: Missing data-processing addendum (DPA) for PII processing — RED FLAG
standard_language: Where Vendor processes Personal Data on behalf of Customer, the GDPR Art. 28-compliant Data Processing Addendum at Schedule X applies, including sub-processor consent process, audit rights, breach notification within 72 hours, and data deletion on termination.
red_flag: Vendor may process personal data as needed to provide the Services. No DPA attached.
why_matters: Processing PII without a DPA violates GDPR Art. 28 (and equivalent laws). Fines up to 4% of global turnover. Standard practice = always attach a DPA when PII is in scope.
suggested_redline: Insert: 'A Data Processing Addendum compliant with GDPR Art. 28 (and equivalent CCPA / UK GDPR / LGPD provisions) is attached as Schedule X and forms part of this Agreement. Sub-processors must be listed and consent-required for additions. Breach notification within 72 hours.'

title: Missing audit rights for security controls — RED FLAG
standard_language: Customer may, upon 30 days' notice, audit Vendor's compliance with the security controls in Schedule Y, no more than once per year (except in case of breach).
red_flag: Vendor's compliance is solely as represented by Vendor; no audit rights.
why_matters: Without audit rights the customer has no recourse if vendor security controls degrade. Standard practice = annual audit, increased in case of breach.
suggested_redline: Insert: 'Customer (or its designated auditor) may, upon 30 days' prior written notice, audit Vendor's compliance with the Security Controls (Schedule Y) once per calendar year, and additionally upon a confirmed Security Incident.'

title: Missing anti-bribery / sanctions warranty — RED FLAG
standard_language: Each party warrants and represents that it complies with the UK Bribery Act 2010, US FCPA, OECD Anti-Bribery Convention, and all applicable sanctions laws (OFAC, UN, EU, HMT). Material breach is grounds for immediate termination without cure period.
red_flag: No anti-bribery clause; no sanctions representation.
why_matters: In regulated industries (financial services, gov contracting), absence of anti-bribery warranty is a critical-tier finding. Audit committees require these clauses in vendor contracts.
suggested_redline: Insert the standard anti-bribery + sanctions warranty above. Adapt to specific regimes if vendor operates in high-risk jurisdictions (e.g. add specific OFAC / UN / EU / HMT designations).

title: Mutual cap on liability
standard_language: EXCEPT FOR (i) breach of confidentiality, (ii) IP infringement, (iii) gross negligence or willful misconduct, and (iv) indemnification obligations, each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed twelve (12) months of fees paid by Customer.
red_flag: Vendor's liability is limited to $10,000 in aggregate, no exceptions. Customer's liability is unlimited.
why_matters: Asymmetric caps strongly favor one party. Standard market practice = mutual caps with the same carve-outs on both sides.
suggested_redline: Replace asymmetric language with: '... each party's aggregate liability ... shall not exceed twelve (12) months of fees paid by Customer.' Apply same carve-outs to both parties.

title: Change-of-control without consent — RED FLAG
standard_language: Neither party may assign this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld). Assignment in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets requires prior notice but not consent.
red_flag: Vendor may freely assign this Agreement to any third party without notice. Customer may not assign without Vendor's consent.
why_matters: Without notice + consent on vendor side, the customer can find itself contractually bound to a competitor or hostile party post-acquisition. Symmetric clause is standard.
suggested_redline: Replace with symmetric clause: 'Neither party may assign without the other's consent, except in connection with a merger / acquisition / sale of substantially all assets, in which case prior notice is required.'

title: Overly-narrow force-majeure clause — RED FLAG (post-COVID)
standard_language: Neither party is liable for failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control, INCLUDING acts of God, war, terrorism, civil unrest, government action, pandemic / epidemic, supply-chain disruption, cyber attack, and power / telecommunications failure.
red_flag: Force-majeure covers only 'acts of God and war.' Pandemic and supply-chain disruption are NOT covered.
why_matters: Post-COVID standard explicitly includes pandemic + supply-chain disruption + cyber attack. Older narrow language exposes the company in foreseeable future disruptions.
suggested_redline: Replace narrow scope with explicit list including pandemic/epidemic, supply-chain disruption (where downstream supplier failure caused the breach), cyber attack, power/telecommunications failure, and government action / sanctions.
